summary: Verified the mobile carousel fix in Banner.jsx and desktop regression. MOBILE (390x844): centred tile is fully on-screen and horizontally centred (imgLeft=36.4, imgRight=353.6 within innerWidth=390, imgCenter=195 vs innerWidth/2=195). Auto-scroll advances ~240px over 4s. Manual scroll works; auto-scroll resumes after touch (~210px in 3.5s). Real article alts observed: RADIOGROEP, 127, KOODH. DESKTOP (1920x800): wide circular sweep still works, auto-scroll advanced ~203px over 4s. No FALLBACK 'Voorbeeldproject' shown. No console errors on either viewport.
critical_code_review_comments: Banner.jsx uses window.innerWidth inside the rAF loop — this is fine but a resize listener toggling isMobile via state would avoid rechecking every frame. Not a blocker.
The rAF loop reads getBoundingClientRect() per panel per frame; acceptable for ~9 panels, but consider memoising if panel count grows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile fix verified on preview URL. Key measurements: mobile centred RADIOGROEP tile imgCenter=195 (== innerWidth/2), scrollTop delta 240px/4s. Desktop scrollTop delta 203px/4s. Alts iterate RADIOGROEP -> 127 -> KOODH. Iterations 1-3 previously covered backend/hover body/title/colours/Pillow 502.
rca of the issue: Original bug: on mobile the desktop transform (translate3d with large x = sin(theta)*R*1.5 where R=viewport height) pushed panels far off-screen horizontally, leaving an empty area and hiding auto-scroll motion. Fix: added an `isMobile = window.innerWidth < 768` branch in the rAF loop that applies only perspective+rotateX tilt + scale/opacity (no horizontal translate/rotateY), keeping tiles centred vertically-stacked so the auto-scroll is clearly visible. Verified working.
